channel.title: Lucid Rob
channel.description: Conspiracies. Hidden history. Signal from the static.

item.title: Tartaria and the Mud Flood: The Conspiracy That Says an Entire Civilization Was Erased
item.description: TikTok found something. Old photos of impossible buildings. Cities supposedly buried in mud. The Tartaria theory says a vast advanced civilization was scrubbed from the history books ... and while a lot of it is pure vibes, a few of the receipts are genuinely hard to wave away.

item.title: Project Blue Beam Explained: The Theory That Says NASA Will Fake an Alien Invasion (And Why It Won't Die)
item.description: Project Blue Beam is the conspiracy that says NASA, the UN, or some shadowy agency is going to stage a fake global alien event using satellites, holograms, and voice-of-God broadcasts. The original 1994 author is dead. The source document reads like a fever dream. And somehow, every UAP hearing makes it trend again. Here's why it won't die.

item.title: CERN Timeline Shifts: Did the Large Hadron Collider Actually Move Us to a Different Reality?
item.description: Every time CERN fires up its main collider, a corner of the internet says we slipped into a parallel universe. The Mandela Effect crowd points at the data. Physicists roll their eyes. Here's what CERN actually does, why the timeline-shift theory keeps trending, and the part of it that's not totally insane.

item.title: What's Really Under the Antarctic Ice: Operation Highjump, Pyramids, and the Continent We're Not Allowed to Visit
item.description: Antarctica is the only continent on Earth where you cannot just buy a plane ticket and visit. Forty-something nations have a treaty governing it. Admiral Byrd's 1947 expedition came back early under fire from a craft that could fly pole to pole at incredible speeds. Here's what's actually under the ice, what's been claimed, and why this one keeps refusing to die.

item.title: The Black Knight Satellite: The Alien Spacecraft Allegedly Orbiting Earth for 13,000 Years
item.description: In 1899 Tesla picked up a strange repeating signal he thought came from off-world. In 1954 a US Navy briefing leaked the name Black Knight for an unidentified object in polar orbit. In 1998 a NASA shuttle mission photographed something dark and irregularly shaped near the ISS. The Black Knight Satellite is what happens when three unrelated weirdnesses get stitched into a single legend.

item.title: Phantom Time Hypothesis: The Theory That Says 297 Years of History Were Made Up
item.description: The Phantom Time Hypothesis says that years 614 to 911 of European history ... including most of Charlemagne's reign ... never actually happened. They were fabricated by the Holy Roman Empire and the Vatican, possibly to inflate calendar numbers around the year 1000. The theory is wrong. Probably. The fact that it's almost impossible to fully disprove is the part that bothers people.

item.title: Bohemian Grove and the Cremation of Care: Inside the Most Powerful Sleepover in America
item.description: Every July, the most powerful men in America ... presidents, CEOs, defense contractors ... gather for two weeks in a redwood grove in Northern California. They do an opening ceremony where they burn an effigy in front of a 40-foot owl statue. Yes, this actually happens. Here's what we know, what's been documented on camera, and what's almost certainly being made up about it.

item.title: HAARP Weather Manipulation: The Alaskan Antenna Array That Conspiracies Say Is Causing Your Hurricane
item.description: Every time a hurricane hits or a wildfire breaks out, somebody on TikTok pulls up a HAARP map and says this isn't an accident. HAARP is real, it's in Alaska, and it's a high-frequency ionospheric research array that the US military used to operate. Here's what it actually does, what it can't do, and why the conspiracy is mostly nonsense ... but not entirely.

item.title: The David Wilcock Suicide Conspiracy: His Final Warning, 48 Hours Before Death
item.description: David Wilcock, the UFO researcher and self-claimed Edgar Cayce reincarnation, died by suicide on April 20, 2026 ... 48 hours after a final livestream where he warned 'people are disappearing, scientists are going missing.' His family says no foul play. The internet disagrees. Here's what's documented and what the conspiracy claims.

item.title: The Missing Scientists Conspiracy: 11 Dead, FBI Investigating
item.description: Eleven US scientists tied to NASA, Los Alamos, and 'exotic' research are dead or missing. The FBI is now investigating. TikTok thinks they were silenced for knowing about 'ultraterrestrials.' Here's what's documented, what the conspiracy claims, and why the boring explanations don't fully cover it.

item.title: The Boston Molasses Flood of 1919: 21 Dead, a Neighborhood Drowned
item.description: On January 15, 1919, a 50-foot steel tank in Boston's North End ruptured and unleashed a 40-foot wave of molasses moving 35 mph. 21 dead, 150 injured, an entire immigrant neighborhood swallowed in syrup. Here's the forgotten American disaster, and the corporate cover-up that followed it.

item.title: The Anunnaki Conspiracy Explained: Did Ancient Sumerian Gods Genetically Engineer Humanity?
item.description: The Anunnaki theory says ancient Sumerian gods were aliens from a hidden planet who genetically engineered humanity hundreds of thousands of years ago to mine gold. Most of it falls apart the second you push on it. Some of it ... weirdly doesn't. Here's the case, the debunking, and why I can't fully let it go.

item.title: Hollow Moon Theory Explained: Why Our Moon Might Actually Be a Spaceship (And Why the Debunks Don't Hold Up)
item.description: The hollow moon theory used to be Soviet fringe speculation. Then you look at the Apollo seismic data, the moon's impossible density, and the isotopic crisis nobody wants to talk about. Here's the case for why our moon might literally be a giant hollow spaceship ... and why I actually think it probably is.

item.title: Artemis II Fake Moon Landing Conspiracy Explained: Why Millions Think NASA Just Faked the Moon Again
item.description: NASA just sent four humans around the moon for the first time since 1972. TikTok has already decided it was AI. Here's why the moon hoax is back. And why it never died.

item.title: Baba Vanga 2026 Alien Spacecraft Prediction Explained: Why TikTok Thinks Aliens Land in November
item.description: A blind Bulgarian mystic who died in 1996 has decided, via TikTok, that aliens are landing in November 2026. Let's actually look at what she said. And what she didn't.

item.title: The Celebrity Clone Conspiracy: Why Millions of People Think Selena Gomez Is a Fake
item.description: TikTok has decided Selena Gomez died in 2017 and got replaced by a clone. She literally made a joke about it on April Fool's Day. And somehow that made people MORE convinced. Let's talk about this.

item.title: Dead Internet Theory Explained: Why the Web Actually Died (And You Didn't Notice)
item.description: The Dead Internet Theory used to be a fringe conspiracy on an obscure forum. Now Reddit's own co-founder says it's real. Here's what's going on and why it matters.

item.title: The Epstein Files Conspiracy Theories Won't Die ... Here's Why (And What We Actually Know)
item.description: Every few months the Epstein files are back in the news. Every few months, the conspiracy theories multiply. Let's separate what's documented from what's vibes.

item.title: AI Deepfakes Explained: How to Spot Fake Videos in 2026 (Before They Cost You)
item.description: AI deepfakes got good. Scary good. If you think you can still tell what's real and what isn't, you're probably already getting fooled. Here's the field guide.

item.title: Is Elvis Still Alive? The Bob Joyce Conspiracy Theory Explained
item.description: An Arkansas pastor named Bob Joyce is apparently Elvis Presley in witness protection. That's the claim. Let's actually take it seriously for thirty seconds.

item.title: NASA Project Anchor Debunked: No, Gravity Is Not Turning Off On August 12
item.description: A viral video claims NASA is warning that Earth will lose gravity for 7 seconds on August 12, 2026. Let me save you the click: that is not how anything works.
